title: SQL Lab 9: Rank Cities by Sales with Ties
short_description: Rank cities by revenue and preserve rank gaps when ties occur.
business_context: Sales leaderboards often need visible rank gaps so tied cities do not hide the number of positions occupied.
problem_statement: Rank cities by revenue descending. If two cities tie, they should share a rank and the next rank should have a gap.
student_task: Write a query returning city, revenue, and city_rank.
learning_goals: Choose RANK when rank gaps matter
production_logs: sales-leaderboard: tied cities received different ranks
review-note: ROW_NUMBER does not model tied leaderboard positions
solution_query:
SELECT city,
       revenue,
       RANK() OVER (ORDER BY revenue DESC) AS city_rank
FROM city_sales;
explanation: RANK gives tied cities the same rank and leaves a gap before the next rank, which matches the prompt.
common_mistakes: Using DENSE_RANK when the prompt expects rank gaps
hints: The prompt says ties share a rank.
The next rank should have a gap.
